DECLARATION OF OLIVIER J. LABARRE IN SUPPORT OF REPLY TO DEMURRER (TRANSACTION ID # 100057724) FILED BY DEFENDANT FAY SERVICING, LLC PHAM, ANH AN INDIVIDUAL BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC. SKYGROUP REALTY, INC.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS FAY SERVICING, LLC, ANH PHAM, BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC., AND SKYGROUP REALTY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADDRESS IN GOOD FAITH ALL ISSUES RAISED BY THE DEMURRER. SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT MUST STATE ALL FACTS AND CAUSES OF ACTION IN CONCISE AND CLEAR LANGUAGE. ALL AMENDMENTS MUST BE IN BOLD FONT. LATE-FILED OVERSIZED OPPOSITION IS STRICKEN. CCP 1005, CCP 1013, CRC 3.1113(D). THE COURT NOTES THAT OPPOSING PARTY AGAIN FAILED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B (SEE SEPTEMBER 19, 2018 MINUTE ORDER). THE COURT ALSO NOTES THAT WHILE COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF INDICATES THAT HER ADDRESS IS IN BEVERLY HILLS, CA (SEE OPPOSITION CAPTION PAGE), COUNSEL DECLARES UNDER THE PENALTY OF PERJURY: "I AM EMPLOYED IN THE COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, STATE OF CALIFORNIA." (SEE PROOF OF SERVICE). PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). THE COURT ALSO ORDERS THE FOLLOWING: OSC RE: WHY A HEARING REGARDING CONTEMPT SHOULD NOT BE SET FOR PLAINTIFF COUNSEL NEDDA HAERI'S (1) FAILURE TO PARTICIPATE IN THE "MEET AND CONFER" PER CCP 430.41; (2) PROVIDING INCONSISTENT INFORMATION UNDER THE PENALTY OF PERJURY TO THE COURT AS TO COUNSEL'S BUSINESS ADDRESS LOCATION; (3) AN ATTEMPT TO ENGAGE IN EX-PARTE COMMUNICATION BY PARTIALLY CONTESTING THE TENTATIVE RULING RE: DISCREPANCY DESCRIBED ABOVE AND IN THE TENTATIVE RULING OVER THE PHONE TWICE (PHONE CALL ON DECEMBER 28, 2018 AND A MESSAGE ON DECEMBER 31, 2018); (4) FAILURE TO ENSURE THE DELIVERY OF FILINGS TO THE OPPOSING PARTY AFTER NOTICE THAT SERVICE WAS NOT RESULTING IN THEIR RECEIPT; (5) FAILURE TO APPEAR FOR DECEMBER 31, 2018 HEARING (THE COURT NOTES THAT TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE WAS REQUESTED IN THE LATE AFTERNOON (4:07 P.M.) ON 12/28/18 AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN POSTED ADDRESSING COURT'S CONCERNS INCLUDING SCHEDULING OF AN OSC RE: CONTEMPT HEARING; NO PRIOR ARRANGEMENT TO ENSURE APPEARANCE ON 12/31/18 [SEE MINUTES FOR ADDED LANG.]

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. ON JUNE 25, 2018, PLAINTIFF FILED AN AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT MOOTS THE INSTANT HEARING. THE COURT GRANTS RELIEF TO PLAINTIFF FROM THE TIMING REQUIREMENTS OF CCP 472. PLAINTIFF SUBMITTED THE AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR FILING IN A TIMELY MANNER ON JUNE 22, 2018, BUT DUE TO THE SIZE OF THE EXHIBITS THE COURT REJECTED THE FILING. PLAINTIFF DILIGENTLY REMEDIED THE ISSUE ON JUNE 25, 2018. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-20-2018 Continued To Feb-20-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-31-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Olivier Labarre (949-477-5050 ext 1036) for Defendant/Moving party FAY SERVICING, LLC, ANH PHAM, BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC., and SKYGROUP REALTY, INC. via CourtCall.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs FAY SERVICING, LLC, ANH PHAM, BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC., and SKYGROUP REALTY, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to address in good faith all issues raised by the demurrer. Second Amended Complaint must state all facts and causes of action in concise and clear language. All amendments must be in bold font. Late-filed oversized opposition is stricken. CCP 1005, CCP 1013, CRC 3.1113(d). The Court notes that opposing party again failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B (See September 19, 2018 minute order). The Court also notes that while counsel for Plaintiff indicates that her address is in Beverly Hills, CA (See Opposition caption page), counsel declares under the penalty of perjury: "I am employed in the County of San Francisco, State of California." (See Proof of Service). Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). The Court also orders the following: OSC Re: Why a hearing regarding Contempt should not be set for Plaintiff counsel Nedda Haeri's (1) failure to participate in the "meet and confer" per CCP 430.41; (2) providing inconsistent information under the penalty of perjury to the Court as to counsel's business address location; (3) an attempt to engage in ex-parte communication by partially contesting the tentative ruling re: discrepancy described above and in the tentative ruling over the phone twice (Phone call on December 28, 2018 and a message on December 31, 2018); (4) failure to ensure the delivery of filings to the opposing party after notice that service was not resulting in their receipt; (5) failure to appear for December 31, 2018 hearing (The Court notes that telephonic appearance was requested in the late afternoon (4:07 p.m.) on December 28, 2018 after the tentative ruling has been posted addressing Court's concerns including scheduling of an OSC Re: Contempt hearing; no prior arrangement to ensure appearance on December 31, 2018 appear to have been made) is set for hearing on February 8, 2019 . Plaintiff's counsel, Nedda Haeri to file a response (in clear and concise language addressing each point under separate heading and including all relevant facts for each heading separately) no later than 4:00 p.m. on February 5, 2019. Judge: Charles F.Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2018 Continued To Dec-31-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-19-2018 Continued To Jan-09-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion Re: Prove Up Hearing Re: Request For Court Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default; Declaration Of Olivier J. Labarre
Off Calendar Oct-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Nedda Haeri of the Advent Law Group, Tel. No. 1-323-580-8998 appearing
for the Plaintiff by telephone appearance;
Atty. Olivier J. Labarre of the Wright Finlay & Zak, LLP, Tel.No. 1-949-477-5050 ext.
1036 appearing for the Defendant BSI Fiancial Services by telephone appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants BSI Financial Services, Inc., Fay Servicing, LLC, Anh Pham and Skygroup Realty, Inc.'s motion to set aside the default entered against them on August 10, 2018 is granted. The August 10, 2018 default is set aside as to all moving defendants per the discretionary relief provisions of CCP section 473(b). Because moving defendants' demurrer to the complaint was not taken off calendar until July 13, 2018, it was reasonable for defendants to believe that they did not need to respond to the first amended complaint until at least thirty days thereafter. Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy favoring deciding cases on their merits and plaintiff 3H Renovation, LLC has not identified any legal prejudice to it by the granting of the motion. The California Supreme Court has stated that "very slight evidence" is required to set aside a default when, as here, the party seeking to do so acts promptly. (Shamblin v. Brattain (1988) 44 Cal 3d 474, 478). Counsel for 3H's conduct in taking moving defendants' default without contacting counsel for the moving defendants to give them advance warning when the day before plaintiffs' counsel had received an email from moving defendants' counsel on a related subjects falls below the standards of civility and fair play expected of all counsel representing parties in California courts. Even if it was proper to take moving defendants' default, 3H should have stipulated to the setting aside of the default without need for this motion to have been filed. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Defendants' demurrer to complaint is OFF CALENDAR. On June 25, 2018, plaintiff filed an amended complaint that moots the instant hearing. The court grants relief to plaintiff from the timing requirements of CCP 472. Plaintiff submitted the amended complaint for filing in a timely manner on June 22, 2018, but due to the size of the exhibits the court rejected the filing. Plaintiff diligently remedied the issue on June 25, 2018.
